title: US Rescue Teams Arrive in Venezuela

source.title: US rescue teams arrive in Venezuela to assist in earthquake response

segments.title: Teams Arrive
segments.audioText: First U.S. rescue teams arrive in Venezuela after deadly earthquakes.
segments.transcript: First U.S. rescue teams arrive in Venezuela after deadly earthquakes.
segments.translationZh: 第一批美國救援隊喺致命地震後抵達委內瑞拉。

points.label: First U.S. rescue teams
points.explanationZh: First 呢度係『第一批』；rescue teams 即救援隊，新聞常用嚟講災後支援。

points.label: arrive in Venezuela
points.explanationZh: arrive in 後面接國家或城市；聽到 arrive 要留意後面地點。

points.label: after deadly earthquakes
points.explanationZh: after 帶出背景原因；deadly earthquakes 即造成傷亡嘅地震。

segments.title: Federal Support
segments.audioText: The U.S. has sent two federal search and rescue teams at max deployment level to Venezuela.
segments.transcript: The U.S. has sent two federal search and rescue teams at max deployment level to Venezuela.
segments.translationZh: 美國已派出兩支聯邦搜救隊，以最高部署級別前往委內瑞拉。

points.label: has sent
points.explanationZh: 現在完成式 has sent 表示『已經派出』，重點係行動已經發生。

points.label: search and rescue teams
points.explanationZh: search and rescue 係固定講法，指搜救工作；聽新聞時可以當成一個 chunk。

points.label: at max deployment level
points.explanationZh: max deployment level 即最高部署級別，語氣比普通派人支援更正式。

segments.title: Fairfax Team
segments.audioText: The Fairfax team said it is sending 80 people and six dogs to Venezuela.
segments.transcript: The Fairfax team said it is sending 80 people and six dogs to Venezuela.
segments.translationZh: Fairfax 團隊表示，會派 80 人同 6 隻搜救犬去委內瑞拉。

points.label: said it is sending
points.explanationZh: said 後面接一個完整資訊；is sending 表示已安排或正在派出。

points.label: 80 people and six dogs
points.explanationZh: 聽數字時先抓 80 people，再抓 six dogs；新聞數字通常係重點。

points.label: to Venezuela
points.explanationZh: to 加目的地，表示人員或物資去邊度。

segments.title: Los Angeles Team
segments.audioText: The County of Los Angeles Fire Department said its team will consist of 71 people and six canines.
segments.transcript: The County of Los Angeles Fire Department said its team will consist of 71 people and six canines.
segments.translationZh: 洛杉磯縣消防局表示，佢哋嘅隊伍會包括 71 人同 6 隻犬隻。

points.label: Fire Department said
points.explanationZh: Fire Department 係消防局；said 後面就係官方回應或資料。

points.label: will consist of
points.explanationZh: consist of 即『由……組成』，比 be made up of 更正式少少。

points.label: six canines
points.explanationZh: canines 係較正式講法，指犬隻；搜救新聞常用嚟講搜救犬。

sentences: First U.S. rescue teams arrive in Venezuela after deadly earthquakes.
The U.S. has sent two federal search and rescue teams at max deployment level to Venezuela.
The Fairfax team said it is sending 80 people and six dogs to Venezuela.
The County of Los Angeles Fire Department said its team will consist of 71 people and six canines.

vocabulary.example: Rescue teams arrived after the earthquake.

vocabulary.translationZh: 地震破壞咗好多建築物。

vocabulary.example: Federal teams helped with the response.
The deployment included people and equipment.
vocabulary.translationZh: 今次部署包括人員同設備。

vocabulary.example: The fire department sent a team.
vocabulary.translationZh: 消防局派出咗一支隊伍。

vocabulary.example: The team will consist of rescue workers and dogs.
vocabulary.translationZh: 隊伍會由救援人員同搜救犬組成。

vocabulary.example: Canines can help search through rubble.
vocabulary.translationZh: 犬隻可以幫手喺瓦礫中搜索。

phrases.example: Rescue teams arrive after the storm.
Search and rescue teams were sent to the area.
The team was sent at max deployment level.
The group will consist of five volunteers.

speakingPractice.promptZh: 由 00:00 開始跟讀 First U.S. rescue teams arrive...，先慢讀 rescue teams arrive，再讀全句。
重播 00:08，聽寫 The U.S. has sent two federal search and rescue teams，再對稿修正。
用 will consist of 練替換：The team will consist of 10 people and two trucks.
用兩句英文複述：U.S. rescue teams arrived in Venezuela. The teams include workers and rescue dogs.

notes: 今日片段係 ABC News 約 1 分 57 秒嘅國際救援新聞，主題係美國搜救隊抵達委內瑞拉協助地震救援。
聽力重點係數字同隊伍資訊：two teams、80 people、six dogs、71 people。
重播時間碼時，先聽主詞，再聽動詞 has sent / said / will consist of，最後補數字。
dailyLifePractice 會轉去較輕嘅日常場景：問最新消息、解釋延誤、請人幫手確認資訊。

dailyLifePractice.title: Asking for an Update
dailyLifePractice.contextZh: 你等緊朋友或職員回覆，想自然又有禮貌咁問最新情況。
dailyLifePractice.audioText: Hi, do you have any update yet? No rush. I just wanted to check in. Thanks, that helps.

lines.speaker: You
lines.en: Hi, do you have any update yet?
lines.zh: 你好，請問有冇最新消息？

lines.speaker: You
lines.en: No rush. I just wanted to check in.
lines.zh: 唔急嘅，我只係想跟進一下。

dailyLifePractice.nativeNoteZh: No rush 係好自然嘅美式口語，可以減低催促感；check in 呢度係『跟進一下』，唔係酒店 check-in。

drills.label: quick repeat
drills.prompt: 跟讀 No rush. I just wanted to check in. 三次，語氣放鬆。
drills.example: No rush. I just wanted to check in.

drills.label: substitution
drills.prompt: 將 update 換成 confirmation、time 或 address。
drills.example: Do you have any confirmation yet?

dailyLifePractice.title: Explaining a Small Delay
dailyLifePractice.contextZh: 你因為交通或安排有變，想簡短通知對方會遲少少。
dailyLifePractice.audioText: Quick heads-up: I'm running a little behind. I should be there in about ten minutes. Thanks for waiting.

lines.speaker: You
lines.en: Quick heads-up: I'm running a little behind.

lines.speaker: You
lines.en: I should be there in about ten minutes.
lines.zh: 我應該大約十分鐘後到。

dailyLifePractice.nativeNoteZh: Quick heads-up 即『先通知你一聲』，好適合輕微延誤。I'm running a little behind 比 I'm late 聽起來柔和啲。

drills.label: say-it-your-way
drills.prompt: 用 I'm running a little behind 講一個你遲到嘅情況。
drills.example: I'm running a little behind, but I'm on my way.

drills.label: short response practice
drills.prompt: 對方話 No worries. Take your time. 你用一句英文回應。

dailyLifePractice.title: Checking Details Together
dailyLifePractice.contextZh: 你同同事或朋友一齊確認資料，想請對方幫手 double-check。
dailyLifePractice.audioText: Can you help me double-check these details? I want to make sure the numbers are right. Sure, let's look at it together.

lines.speaker: You
lines.en: Can you help me double-check these details?
lines.zh: 你可唔可以幫我再確認一次啲資料？

lines.speaker: Coworker
lines.en: Sure, what should I look at?

lines.speaker: You
lines.en: I want to make sure the numbers are right.
lines.zh: 我想確保啲數字係啱嘅。

lines.speaker: Coworker
lines.en: Sure, let's look at it together.

dailyLifePractice.nativeNoteZh: double-check 係非常常用嘅口語同工作英文，表示『再核對一次』。I want to make sure... 聽起來謹慎但唔怪責人。

drills.label: substitution
drills.prompt: 用 I want to make sure... 換成時間、地址、名字。
drills.example: I want to make sure the address is right.

drills.label: role-play
drills.prompt: 你做 You，請對方幫你確認一項資料。
drills.example: Can you help me double-check the time?
